rationale: All three lenses ran successfully against the spec and implementation diff. One major finding: the spec explicitly required updating the existing 'Project filter applies correctly' test in WorkflowsListPage.integration.test.tsx with non-null display_name fixtures and a chip-label assertion; this was not done. The core feature implementation is correct — all three filter components wire useProjectDisplayNames, and the three new restore-path integration tests pass. The WorkflowsFilters persistedProject stabilization is an acceptable divergence from the literal spec wording (it prevents an IIFE render loop). Deferred: EC-001 (mid-session non-roster selection after clearing search shows technical name — same behavior as before the fix, pre-existing limitation not introduced by this change); ACC-002 (missing v1/assistants mock in AssistantsListPage tests — tests pass and assertion is independent of assistants list load). Dismissed: EC-002 (filters.project ?? [] reference stability — no functional impact); blind-lens minors (fallback-state coverage on Skills, direct cache mutation in tests).

standards_review.notes: All three commits follow EPMCDME-13520: Capital sentence pattern enforced by Tekton CI regex
ESLint clean after nested-ternary fix (no-nested-ternary in WorkflowsFilters.tsx:66 resolved by replacing ternary chain with early return)
No security issues — display name resolution reads from a trusted internal API endpoint (v1/projects/{name}) with existing auth patterns; no user-controlled data rendered unsanitized

findings.problem: WorkflowsListPage 'Project filter applies correctly' test not updated per spec — display_name: null fixtures retained, no chip-label assertion added
findings.impact: The spec-required regression guard for the interactive-filter-selection path is absent. If resolvedProjectOptions broke in a way that still falls back to technical names for dropdown-selected projects (e.g. extras logic has a bug), this test would not catch it. The new restore-path test covers the refresh case only.
findings.recommendation: Update the three v1/projects mock responses in 'Project filter applies correctly' (display_name: null → a real string such as 'Project A Display'). Update the selectMultiSelectOptions call to use the display names. Add a waitFor assertion that the applied chip label equals the display name. This is mechanically the same pattern as the restore-path test added for Workflows — just within the existing test body.
